criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose complete left bundle branch block, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of a normal QRS duration
Presence of ST-segment depression in anterior leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is the QRS duration prolonged on this ECG? Note that a QRS duration is considered to be prolonged if it is greater than 120 milliseconds.

grounding.question: In which of the following leads can you observe a QRS wave that demonstrates the prolonged QRS duration? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured QRS duration fall into?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have complete left bundle branch block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose complete left bundle branch block?
options: Presence of pathological Q waves in leads V1 and V2
Presence of a Delta wave (Slurred upstroke)
Presence of a dominant S wave in leads V1 and V2
Presence of a dominant R wave in leads V1 and V2
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of a dominant S wave in leads V1 and V2

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, Does this ECG show dominant S waves in leads V1 and V2, where the amplitude of the negative deflection in the QRS complex is greater than the amplitude of the positive deflection?

grounding.question: Within the leads V1 and V2, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with dominant S wave? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds. The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have complete left bundle branch block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the findings you have identified so far, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose complete left bundle branch block?
options: Presence of a positive QRS complex in lateral leads
Presence of negative QRS complexes in lateral leads
Presence of ST-segment depression in anterior leads
Presence of pathological Q waves in lateral leads
Presence of low voltage QRS complexes in lateral leads
answer: Presence of a positive QRS complex in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show positive QRS complexes in at least two of leads I, aVL, and V6?

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show the positive QRS complexes? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the positive QRS complexes? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have complete left bundle branch block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the findings you have identified so far, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose complete left bundle branch block?
options: Presence of biphasic T waves in lateral leads
Presence of a notched QRS complex in lateral leads
Presence of ST-segment depression in anterior leads
Presence of pathological Q waves in lateral leads
Presence of a slurred S wave in lateral leads
answer: Presence of a notched QRS complex in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show notched QRS complexes in any of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6?

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show the notched QRS complexes? Select all possible leads from the options below.
